summary: Surah At-Takwir depicts apocalyptic signs: the sun folded up, stars falling, mountains driven, wild beasts gathered, seas ablaze, records opened, Hell enflamed, and Paradise brought near—each soul will know its deeds. It condemns infanticide, affirms that the Qur’an is conveyed by the honored, trustworthy angel Jibril, and that the Prophet is not insane. The Qur’an is not from Satan; it is a universal reminder for whoever wills to be upright—yet will is under Allah’s will.

Section: Authenticity of revelation: Qur’an conveyed by a noble, mighty, obeyed, trustworthy messenger (Jibril); your companion is not mad; he saw him on the clear horizon; not satanic speech; guidance for whoever wills—by Allah’s will
